details.description: A Dark Wind Covers Arkham is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The terror level increases by 1. The maximum Stamina of all investigators in Arkham is reduced by 1. Horror checks in Arkham are made at a -2 penalty.
details.flavor_text: Inky clouds blow in from the east, bringing with them sickness and fear.

name: A New Dawn!
gate_location: Esoteric Order of Dagon or The Witch House

details.description: A New Dawn! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Each investigator who is currently at 2 or less Sanity gains 1 Sanity. Each investigator who is currently at 2 or less Stamina gains 1 Stamina.

details.description: A Sandstorm!? is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators must spend 2 additional movement points to enter any location marked with a green diamond. While this card is in play, instead of having an Arkham Encounter, any investigator in a street area may pass a Luck (-1) check to draw 1 Exhibit Item.
details.flavor_text: Against all reason, a sandstorm comes up out of nowhere and strikes Arkham, clogging the streets and burying whole buildings! Strangely, it seems to avoid certain places with dark reputations.

details.mythos_ability: Investigators cannot gain Stamina except by receiving medical care in St. Mary's Hospital (or from Vincent Lee).

details.description: Aldeberan Ascendant is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Flying creatures do not move to the sky. Gates to Another Dimension cannot be closed or sealed.

details.description: Alien Technology is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Mi-Go have their toughness increased by 2. If an investigator passes a Combat check against a Mi-Go, he draws 1 extra Unique Item.

details.description: All Quiet in Arkham! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Each player may pass a Luck (-1) check to be Blessed.

sections.Clarification: Despite the name of the card, investigators in other worlds too may try to pass the check.

details.description: Along Came Jones is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first investigator who ends his movement in the Train Station draws 1 Ally, then discards this card from play.

details.description: Amnesiac Professor's Memory Returns! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Place 2 Clue tokens in each location with an elder sign.

details.description: An Evil Fog is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Will checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Sneak checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. Fliers do not move.

details.description: Angry Red Skies is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Will checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Lore checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. Fliers do not move to or from the Sky.

details.description: Another Time, Another Place! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Every open gate leading to Another Dimension or Another Time releases one monster into its location.

details.description: Balmy is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Sneak checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Fight checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. When moving, stalkers move to investigators in street areas or unstable locations up to 2 spaces away.

details.description: Bank Foreclosure is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Any investigators in Arkham with Bank Loans must pay them off immediately. You may sell items at half their list price (round down) if you do not have the $10; if you cannot or will not pay, you are arrested.
details.flavor_text: An archaeological expedition to Egypt sponsored by the bank has disappeared without a trace ... which means no returns on the bank's investment. Concerned for their savings, townsfolk rush the bank to withdraw their funds.

name: Bank Targeted By Grifters
gate_location: Cold Spring Glen or Hibb's Roadhouse

details.description: Bank Targeted By Grifters is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 1 Clue token on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play).
details.pass_condition: If an investigator who either is the Deputy of Arkham or has a Sheldon Gang Membership (from the Dunwich Horror expansion) discards 3 Clue tokens while at the Bank of Arkham, return this card to the box. Each investigator then gains $10.
details.fail_condition:
If there are 6 Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. Each investigator must discard all of his money and any Retainer cards.

Activity At: Bank of Arkham

details.description: Big Storm Sweeps Arkham! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Sky and Outskirts are returned to the cup.

details.description: Bizarre Couriers is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators in Arkham may immediately trade items or money without being in the same location. However, each investigator involved in the transaction loses 1 Sanity per item given or received.
details.flavor_text: They come bearing strange messages, wrapped in ragged cloaks and hiding their pale flesh. Those who receive packages from them shudder at the memory of them, saying that they have the appearance of "plump, white grave worms."

details.description: Bizarre Dreams Plague Citizens! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All Gugs and Nightgaunts in Arkham are returned to the cup. If at least one monster returns to the cup, raise the terror level by 1.

details.description:
Black Sabbath! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the
Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All Rat-things, Witches, and Warlocks in Arkham are returned to the cup. If at least one monster returns to the cup, raise the terror level by 1.

details.description:
Blackest Night is a mythos card. It first appeared in the
Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Luck checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Sneak checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus.

details.description: Blood Magic is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in Rivertown streets may choose to delve into dark mysteries using their life force. They roll dice equal to their current Stamina, and for every failed die, they lose 1 Stamina. If this reduces them to 0 Stamina, they are devoured and the player must start a new character. Otherwise, they gain 3 Clue tokens.

details.description: Blue Flu! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All investigators in jail are released. No investigators may be arrested until the end of next turn. Leave this card in play until then to indicate this.

details.description: Blue Plate Special is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in Velma's Diner may spend up to $4 instead of having an Arkham encounter. They gain 1 Stamina and 1 Sanity for every $1 spent.

details.description: Blue Skies is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All Investigators receive -1 Sneak, +1 Speed.

details.description: Book Sale is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the Library may spend $2 instead of having an Arkham encounter. Any investigator that does so then takes the first Tome from the Unique Items deck.

details.description: Brawling in the Streets is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Any investigator in a street area at the end of the Mythos phase must make a Fight (+0) check. If he fails and the terror level is 3 or less, he loses 1 Stamina. If he fails the check and the terror level is 4 or higher, he loses 2 Stamina.
details.clue_locations: Esoteric Order of Dagon or The Unnamable

details.description: Break-In at Neil's is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The thieves carelessly break a dangerous artifact, releasing 2 monsters into the South Shore streets.

details.description: Brutal Accident! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first player must choose one investigator (even himself). That investigator loses 3 Stamina.

details.description: Campus Security Increased! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Miskatonic U. Streets or Locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Cash on the Hoof! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first player finds $2 lying on the ground.

name: Charity Auction!
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Charity Auction! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: Deal a number of cards from the top of the Common Item deck equal to the number of players. Each player may purchase one item at its listed cost plus $1. Discard any items not purchased.

details.description: Charity Case! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The investigator with the lowest Sanity (the first player breaks ties) may move to Arkham Asylum and be delayed to restore his Sanity to its maximum.

name: Checkered Cab Trials Begin!
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Checkered Cab Trials Begin! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: All investigators in Arkham may move directly to any street location in Arkham as their entire move (ignore all monsters between their current location and their destination).
details.flavor_text: Arkham experiments with mass transit.

details.description: Church Boycotts Exhibit is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Lore checks in Arkham are made at a -2 penalty. Will checks in Arkham are made at a +2 bonus.
details.flavor_text: Father Michael organizes his congregation against the "Legacy of the Pharaohs" museum exhibit, calling the displays "ghastly and an affront against the Lord."

details.description: Church Group Reclaims Southside! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Southside streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Citizens Urged to Lock Doors is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Monsters appear in the Outskirts until the Outskirts limit is reached. If the Outskirts already contains the maximum number of monsters, return all of the monsters in the Outskirts to the monster cup and increase the terror level by 1.

details.description: City Gripped by Blackouts! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: The General Store, Curiositie Shoppe, and Ye Olde Magick Shoppe are closed until the end of next turn. Leave this card in play until then to indicate this.

details.description: Closed Mouths is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, Clue tokens do not appear on the board when mythos cards are resolved.

details.description: Clothing Drive is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, investigators without Exhibit Items must evade or fight a Mummy in each street location they enter. These Mummies have the same stats as Zombies except with a toughness of 2, and cannot be taken as trophies.
details.flavor_text: Father Michael sends out a call for clothing. "Have you seen our streets? The homeless have been reduced to wearing rags and dirty strips!" In fact, the beings he has seen on the street are mummies, come to life and escaped from the visiting museum exhibit!

details.description: Compass Needles Point South! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first player chooses two investigators. Those investigators immediately exchange locations, ignoring all monsters and movement restrictions.

name: Construction Crew Conundrum!
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Construction Crew Conundrum! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: A monster appears at the Downtown streets and the Miskatonic U. streets.
details.flavor_text: While digging, a work crew uncovers ancient artifacts ... and ancient monsters.

name: Contagion Kills Life in Blasted Heath!

details.page_title: Contagion Kills Life in Blasted Heath!
details.description: Contagion Kills Life in Blasted Heath! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Blasted Heath streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Cops Sweep Kingsport! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in Kingsport street areas or locations are returned to the monster cup.

details.description: Corpse Garden is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the Central Hill streets gain 2 Clue tokens, then lose 1 Sanity and 1 Stamina.

details.description: Corpses Preserved is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Monster trophies come to life! Each investigator in Arkham must randomly choose one of his collected monster trophies. That monster surprises him. If the investigator defeats the monster, he may draw cards from the Exhibit Item deck equal to its toughness, take one, and discard the rest. Only monsters that are defeated may be taken as trophies again; if the investigator flees or the monster defeats him, the monster is returned to the box.

sections.Errata: The last line should say that the monster is returned to the cup, not to the box.

details.description: Cosmic Upheaval! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators that are currently in Other Worlds must immediately pass a Luck (-1) check or be lost in time and space.

details.description: Couriers in Town! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Until the end of next turn, investigators may trade Items with one another without being in the same space. Leave this card in place to signify this. Items may only be traded in this manner during Upkeep.

details.description: Cover-up is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: When this card enters play, place 3 Clue tokens per player on it. Any player may discard monster trophies while in the Downtown streets during the Arkham Encounters Phase to remove 1 Clue token from this card for each toughness worth of monsters discarded.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 1 Clue token per player on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play).
details.pass_condition: If there are no Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. Each investigator is Blessed, and gains a Retainer.
details.fail_condition: If there are 6 Clue tokens per player on this card, return it to the box. All investigators lose all of their Clue tokens, and all Clue tokens are removed from the board.

details.description: Curfew Enforced is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Any investigator who ends his movement in the streets must pass a Will (+0) check or be arrested and taken to the Police Station.

sections.Clarification: Curfew Enforced does not apply to The Deputy of Arkham.

details.description: Cursed Relics is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Place 5 doom tokens on this card when it comes into play.
details.ongoing_effect: At the end of every Mythos Phase after the first, move 1 doom token from this card to the terror track and increase the terror level by 1. Any player may exhaust a ready Exhibit Item or discard a Unique Item to negate both effects.
details.pass_condition: If there are no doom tokens on the terror track after the last doom token is removed from this card, discard this card. Each player draws 1 Spell.
details.fail_condition: If there are doom tokens on the terror track after the last doom token is removed from this card, discard this card and all doom tokens from the terror track. The terror level rises by a number equal to the number of doom tokens discarded.

details.description: Dark Conjunction is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, all monsters gain Overwhelming 1. Any monster that already has Overwhelming has its Overwhelming rating increased by 1.

details.description: Dark Day! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators cannot spend Clue tokens to add to skill checks until the end of next turn. Leave this card in play until then as a reminder.

details.description: Dark Shapes at Dusk! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All Ghasts, Leng Spiders, and Moon-beasts are returned to the cup. If at least one monster returns to the cup, raise the terror level by 1.

details.description: Dark Skies is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Fight checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Sneak checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. Stalkers move like normal monsters.

details.description: Darke's Carnival Arrives is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the Northside streets gain 1 Clue token from the sinister wonders they witness, but must pass a Will (-1) check or lose 1 Sanity.

details.description: Deathly Still is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Will checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Fight checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. Monsters that move normally are stalkers.

details.description: Dire Portents Abound is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators receive -1 to checks to close Gates.

details.description: Disturbing the Dead is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.ongoing_effect: Roll a die at the end of every Mythos Phase while this card is in play (beginning the turn after it entered play). On a 1 or 2, increase the terror level by 1.
details.pass_condition: If a single player discards 2 gate trophies during the Arkham Encounter Phase while in the Rivertown streets, return this card to the box. Each player draws 1 Spell.
details.fail_condition: If the terror level reaches 10, return this card to the box. Every investigator is Cursed.

name: Double the Trouble
gate_location: Unvisited Isle and The Witch House

details.description: Double the Trouble is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Two Gates open this turn, but no doom tokens are added to the doom track.

details.description: Dreams of a Sunken City is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators cannot gain Sanity except by receiving psychiatric care at Arkham Asylum (or from Carolyn Fern).

details.description: Dunwich Villagers Drive Out Undesirables! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Village Commons streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Early Frost is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability:
As they slip and fall on the ice, each investigator loses 1 Stamina for each movement point over 3 he spends each turn.
Reading Tomes does not count towards this total.

details.description: Eccentric Opens Doors is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the French Hill streets may discard 1 Gate trophy to draw 1 Unique Item

name: Eerie Coincidences
gate_location: Independence Square and The Witch House

details.description: Eerie Coincidences is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Two Gates open this turn, but no doom tokens are added to the doom track.

details.description: Egyption Exhibit Visits Miskatonic U. is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Any investigator who ends his movement in the Miskatonic U. streets may pass a Lore (-1) check to gain 1 Clue token by reading the strange hieroglyphics on the artifacts in the exhibit.

name: Endlessly Breeding
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Endlessly Breeding is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.ongoing_effect: At the end of each Mythos phase, draw a second Mythos card and ignore everything on it except for its monster movement pattern. Each time an investigator is reduced to 0 Stamina or 0 Sanity, place a Clue token on this card.
details.pass_condition: If a single investigator discards $10 while at the Riverside Docks, return this card to the box. Starting with the first player, each player chooses a monster in play and takes it as a monster trophy (ignoring Endless).
details.fail_condition: If there are ever 3 Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. A monster surge occurs, then add a doom token to the doom track for each Hex monster in play.

details.description: Escape from Arkham Asylum! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability:
Collect up to 3 Maniacs, drawing them first from the monster cup, then from the Outskirts, then from those claimed as trophies. Place them all in the Downtown streets.

Investigators who defeat all 3 Maniacs before the end of next turn may draw 1 Exhibit Item. Leave this card in play until then to indicate this.
details.flavor_text: The inmates at the Asylum all begin chanting in an ancient language, and overpower the orderlies! "Nephren-Ka! Nephren-Ka!" The streets flood with the insane.

details.description: Estate Sale is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the Uptown streets may draw 2 Unique Items and purchase one, none, or both of them at list price, discarding any that are not purchased.

details.description: Evidence Destroyed! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: A massive conspiracy sets back the investigation. Discard all Clue tokens from the board.

details.description: Family Found Butchered! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: The terror level increases by 1 in light of this tragic news.

details.description: Feds Raid Arkham! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the streets are returned to the cup.

sections.Clarification: This event does not apply to Spawn monsters.

gate_location: Gardners' Place or The Witch House

details.clue_locations:

* Cold Spring Glen or Black Cave

details.description: Free Clinic! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The investigator with the lowest Stamina (the first player breaks ties) may move to St. Mary's Hospital and be delayed to restore his Stamina to its maximum.

expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: All monsters have their Sanity damage increased by 1.

details.description: Fund Drive for the Arts is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators in Arkham may immediately spend up to $1 for every Ally left in the Ally deck. For every $2 an investigator spends, he may draw one card from the Unique Item deck. He then takes one card from among those he has drawn and shuffles the rest back into the deck.
details.flavor_text: The performance of the controversial "The King in Yellow" have increased local enthusiasm for the arts, and the Historical Society is taking advantage of it with a fundraising auction.

name: Funnel Clouds
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Funnel Clouds is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability:
Any investigator who ends his movement in the streets must pass a Luck (+1) check or be devoured.

All flying monsters are returned to the monster cup.

details.description: Gangs Clean Up Easttown! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Easttown streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Gathering Storm! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The situation in Innsmouth continues to worsen. Add one uprising token to the Deep Ones Rising track.

details.description: Ghost Ship Docks by Itself! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: An ancient ghost ship arrives in Arkham, releasing 2 monsters into the Merchant Dist. streets.

details.description: Goat-like Creature Spotted in Woods! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All Dark Young in Arkham are returned to the cup. If at least one monster returns to the cup, raise the terror level by 1.

details.description: Good Traveling Conditions is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Fight checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Speed checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus, and investigators receive 1 extra movement point. Monsters that move normally are fast.

details.mythos_ability: When this card enters play, place 6 Clue tokens on it. Any player may spend Clue tokens during the Arkham Encounter Phase while in the Easttown streets to discard Clue tokens from this card on a 1-for-1 basis.
details.ongoing_effect: Roll two dice at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play). For every 1 or 2 rolled, place one Clue token on this card.

details.fail_condition: If there are 10 Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. All elder sign tokens are removed from the board.

details.description: Graverobbers Strike! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Unfortunately for them, they release 2 monsters into the Central Hill streets.

expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the Southside Streets may roll a die. On a success, they gain $2. On a failure, they lose $1.

details.description: Hallelujah! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first investigator who ends his movement in South Church is Blessed, then discards this card from play.

details.description: Happy Days are Here Again is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Due to the renewed prosperity that has come to Arkham, the terror level cannot increase.

details.description: Heat Wave is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Fight checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Lore checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. Fire Vampires have their toughness increased by 1.

details.description: Hellene Sighted! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The ancient ship briefly docks, releasing 2 monsters into the Harborside streets.

details.description: Help Wanted is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first investigator without a Retainer who ends his movement at the Newspaper gains a Retainer, then discards this card from play.

details.description: High Tides is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators in Rivertown, Easttown, and Merchant District streets or locations must pass a Fight (-1) Check or be delayed.
details.flavor_text: The tides seem to be defying the tidal charts, causing elevated water levels far upriver.

name: Horns of the Black Goat
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Horns of the Black Goat is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: Dark Young have their toughness increased by 1.

details.description: Horrifying Sight! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first player must choose one investigator (even himself). That investigator loses 3 Sanity.

details.description: Horror at Groundbreaking! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: An ancient stone is disturbed by the construction, releasing 2 monsters into the Miskatonic U. streets.

name: Hospital Patients Disappear!
gate_location: Wizard's Hill or The Witch House

details.description: Hospital Patients Disappear! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All investigators who have an Injury card (from the Dunwich Horror expansion) or whose Stamina is currently 2 or less move immediately to the first area of Another Dimension.

details.description: Icy Conditions is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators receive 1 less movement point during the Movement Phase. Fast Monsters move like normal monsters.

details.description: Ill Wind Grips Arkham! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: The first player must pass a Luck (-1) check or be Cursed

sections.Clarification: Despite the name of the card, the first player must pass the check even if he currently is in an other world.

name: Impassable Streets
gate_location: Esoteric Order of Dagon or Unvisited Isle

details.description: Impassible Streets is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: An investigator may not move to a street area if there is a black movement arrow leading to it from his present street area. This does not effect moving from a location to a street area or from a street area to a location.

details.description: Infestation of Rats is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Each investigator in Ma's Boarding House or Velma's Diner at the end of the Mythos Phase, as well as each investigator in First National Grocery, Gilman House Hotel, or Innsmouth Jail on the Innsmouth Horror expansion game board at the end of the Mythos Phase, loses 1 Sanity.

details.description: Innsmouth Invasion! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The city of Innsmouth is suddenly crawling with monsters. A monster appears in the Church Green streets, the Innsmouth Shore streets, the Factory District streets, Sawbone Alley, and Devil Reef.

details.description: Innsmouth is Restless is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, investigators who end their movement in Innsmouth receive a -1 penalty to their Evade check to avoid being arrested once martial law is declared in Innsmouth.

details.description: Innsmouth Sleeps is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, investigators who end their movement in Innsmouth do not need to make the usual Evade check to avoid being arrested once martial law is declared in Innsmouth.

details.description: Intermission is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability:
The town of Arkham collectively holds its breath as the terrible play "The King in Yellow" builds to a crescendo, preparing to reveal its darkest horrors.
But for the moment, nothing happens. No gates open, but the terror level is increased by 2.

details.description: Jail Break! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: However, it appears that whatever was being held in the jail wasn't quite human, so 2 monsters are released into the Church Green streets.

details.description: King of Cards Cancels Show is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All Witches and Warlocks in Arkham are returned to the cup. If any investigator has Eric Weiss as an Ally, that investigator gains those monsters as trophies.
details.flavor_text: Erich Weiss, a famous visiting escape artist and master of illusion, was scheduled to put on a show. However, when he was told that the "Legacy of the Pharaohs" museum exhibit was in town, he paled visibly and canceled the performance. "There is a thing I must do" was his only comment.

details.description: Kingsport Mists is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Sneak checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. The Causeway, the Wireless Station, and the Strange High House in the Mists are closed while this card remains in play.

details.description: Lightning Storm is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: At the start of each Upkeep Phase while this card is in play, roll a die. On a 1 or 2, the first player must choose an investigator in a street area (if there are any). That investigator loses a die of Stamina and moves to St. Mary's Hospital.

name: Lightning Strikes Twice!
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Lightning Strikes Twice! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: Two similar rocks are shattered by lightning strikes in different parts of Arkham, each releasing a monster. A monster appears at the French Hill streets and the Uptown streets.

details.description: Local Heiress Missing! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: If any player has Asenath Waite as an Ally, she is returned to the box and a doom token is added to the Ancient One's doom track.

details.description: Lodge Member Held for Questioning! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: A Silver Lodge ritual lets 2 monsters loose in the French Hill streets.

details.description: Lodge Members Watch the Night! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the French Hill streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Lynch Mob! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: One Ally is randomly drawn from the Ally deck and returned to the box.

details.description: Mad Bomber is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: When this card enters play, place $4 per player on it. Any player may pay money while in the Merchant District streets during the Arkham Encounters Phase to remove money from this card on a 1-for-1 basis.
details.ongoing_effect: Place $1 per player on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play).
details.pass_condition: If there is no money on this card, return it to the box. Each player gains 2 Clue tokens.
details.fail_condition: If there is $8 per player on this card, return it to the box. Every Investigator must immediately draw 1 Injury and 1 Madness card.

details.description: Manhunt in Arkham! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Medical Supplies Contaminated is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: When an investigator pays $2 to use the "Medical Care" ability at St. Mary's Hospital, he loses 1 Sanity. When an investigator pays $2 to use the "Psychiatric Care" ability at Arkham Asylum, he loses 1 Stamina.
details.clue_locations: Cold Spring Glen or Unvisited Isle

details.description: Merchants March on Crime! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Merchant Dist. streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Meteorite Spotted in Blasted Heath! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: A crashed meteorite releases 2 monsters into the Blasted Heath streets.

details.description: Mirages of Lakes is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: When investigators move from one street to another, they may only move in the direction of the black arrows. Locations may be entered normally.
details.flavor_text: It's called the "Hali Effect." It is similar to seeing mirages in the desert, but it only occurs in urban environments. Entire cities claim to see vast lakes bordering their neighborhoods, where just the day before was dry land. Sometimes the mirage recedes as the viewer walks forward... sometimes it does not.

details.description: Miskatonic Arctic Expedition Returns! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Any Elder Things previously claimed as monster trophies by players return to life and are placed in the River Docks.

sections.Mythos Source: The Miskatonic Arctic Expedition is the subject of H. P. Lovecraft's classic novella 'At the Mountains of Madness' (1936) in which the Elder Things first appear.

details.description: Miskatonic Launches New Research Ship! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Place 2 Clue tokens in the River Docks, Unvisited Isle, as well as all aquatic locations and all aquatic street areas on expansion game boards.

details.description: Miskatonic Scientists Research Anomalies! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Place 2 Clue tokens in the Science Building as well as all street areas and locations adjacent to a vortex.

details.description: Missing People Return! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All investigators currently lost in time and space immediately return to Arkham, appearing in a street or location of their choice.

details.description: Mistrust is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: When this card enters play, place 3 Clue tokens per player on it. Any player may discard Common or Unique Items while in the Southside streets during the Arkham Encounters Phase to remove 1 Clue token per Common or 2 per Unique Item discarded.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 1 Clue token per player on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play).
details.pass_condition: If there are no Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. Each investigator gains an Ally.
details.fail_condition: If there are 6 Clue tokens per player on this card, return it to the box. Investigators may no longer trade items and all investigators have their Focus reduced to 0 for the rest of the game.

details.description: Moon Creature Hoax is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first player draws a monster marker from the cup at random and places it in the Sky. Treat this monster as a flying monster until it moves to a street area. After that, the monster's movement is treated normally.

details.description: Mudslides is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: During the Mythos Phase, each player whose investigator is in a street area must roll a die. If the result is a 1, the investigator must move to the street area that is reached by following the white arrow from his current street area. If the white arrow leads to a vortex or an area with a gate, the investigator stays in his current street area instead.
details.clue_locations:
* 7th House on the Left
* Devil's Hopyard or The Witch House

details.description: Murder in the Museum is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Players in Arkham with gate trophies must make a Fight check or Lore check for each trophy they have, with a modifier equal to the modifier printed on the gate. If a check for a gate is failed, draw monsters from the cup until you draw a monster with a symbol that matches the one on the gate, and place that monster in the player's current location. If the check for a gate succeeds, the player may remove one monster with that gate's symbol from Arkham, the Outskirts, or the Sky.
details.flavor_text: The victims of a ritual murder are found at the "Legacy of the Pharaohs" museum exhibit. The professors brought in from Miskatonic believe that it was an opening ritual.

name: Murderer at Large; Victims' Brains Missing

details.page_title: Murderer at Large; Victims' Brains Missing
details.description: Murderer at Large; Victims' Brains Missing is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The terror level increases by 1.
details.flavor_text: Victims of a string of murders are left as desiccated corpses with their brains and internal organs removed.

details.description: Museum Artifacts Stolen is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators in Arkham may pay $5 in bribe fees to make a connection with the robbers. Those who do so may draw a number of Exhibit Items equal to their focus, choose one, and discard the rest.

details.description: Museum Haunted is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The ghosts of the pharaohs walk abroad! Collect up to 2 Ghosts, drawing them first from the monster cup, then from the Outskirts, then from those claimed as trophies. Place them in the Miskatonic U. streets. Investigators who destroy one of these Ghosts before the end of next turn may draw one Exhibit Item. Leave this card in play until then to indicate this. The terror level increases by 1.

name: Mysterious Crates Found Empty!
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Mysterious Crates Found Empty! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: But the monsters inside them have already escaped. A monster appears at the Riverside streets and the Northside streets.

details.description: Mysterious Lights Fade! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The investigators may collectively spend 5 Clue tokens. If they do so, the first player may choose and close 1 open Gate in any Arkham location and reshuffle it into the stack of Gate markers (he does not gain a Gate trophy). The investigators may not do this if there is only 1 open Gate.

details.description: Neighborhood Sale is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the Easttown streets may draw 2 Common Items and purchase one, none, or both of them at list price, discarding any that are not purchased.

details.description: New Miskatonic U. Curriculum is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: If an investigator reads a Tome in the Library, he gains a +2 bonus to any Lore check made to do so.
details.flavor_text: "The King in Yellow" has, amidst heavy controversy, been added to the library curriculum at the University. Its teaching has stirred up the students' interest in all written occult works.

name: New Moon
gate_location: Esoteric Order of Dagon or Woods

details.description: New Moon is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators receive 1 less movement point (to a minimum of 0) during the Movement Phase. When an investigator is driven insane, he does not discard any of his Clue tokens.

details.description: Nightmare Pool is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: When this card enters play, place 3 Sanity tokens per player on it. Any player may pay Sanity while in the Uptown streets during the Arkham Encounters Phase to remove Sanity tokens from this card on a one-for-one basis.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 1 Sanity token per player on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play).
details.pass_condition: If there are no Sanity tokens on this card, return it to the box. Each player draws 1 Common Item.
details.fail_condition: If there are 6 Sanity tokens per player on this card, return it to the box. Immeditately draw and resolve 3 more mythos cards, one after the other.

name: No One Can Help You Now

details.page_title: No One Can Help You Now
details.description: No One Can Help You Now is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Gates cannot be sealed, although they can still be closed.

details.description: Noden's Favor is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: It costs 2 fewer Clue tokens to seal gates.

details.description: Noted Author Dies is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All players must discard 1 Clue token.

details.description: Off Broadway? is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All purchases in Arkham cost $1 more than usual.
details.flavor_text: The performances of the new play "The King in Yellow" are garnering rave reviews. Theater professors at Miskatonic University claim never to have heard of it, but nonetheless, the wealthy and influential from all around New England are flocking to see it. The shops have, of course, raised their prices to take advantage of this influx of money.

details.description: Old Building Collapses! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Unfortunately, the building was containing 2 monsters, which have now been released into the Factory District streets.

details.description: Old Debts Come Due! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Raise the terror level by 1. Any investigator who has previously made a deal with the Dark Man of Wizard's Hill is devoured. For every investigator devoured in this way, place 3 doom tokens on the doom track. All current debts to the Dark Man are now considered paid.

name: One Thousand Young
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: One Thousand Young is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.ongoing_effect: When a monster enters Uptown Streets, place it on this card. Monsters on this card do not count against the monster limit.
details.pass_condition: Investigators who end their movement in the Uptown Streets may make a Lore (-1) check to place a clue token on this card. If there are as many clue tokens on this card as there are investigators plus one, return it to the box (and all monsters on it to the cup) and each investigator gains a random monster trophy.
details.fail_condition: If there are as many monsters on this card as there are investigators plus one, return it to the box (and all monsters on it to the cup). All investigators are devoured.

details.description: Oppressive Atmosphere is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, all monsters gain Nightmarish 1. Any monster that already has Nightmarish has its Nightmarish rating increased by 1.

details.description: Order of Dagon Suspected! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Although no proof exists, it is suspected that the Esoteric Order of Dagon released 2 monsters into the Church Green streets.

name: Parazoologist Visits Miskatonic U.
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Parazoologist Visits Miskatonic U. is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the Miskatonic U. Streets may discard 5 Toughness worth of monster trophies to gain 1 Spell.

name: Patient Claims Madness Caused by Words!

details.page_title: Patient Claims Madness Caused by Words!
details.description: Patient Claims Madness Caused by Words! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Each investigator with a Tome loses 2 Sanity. Each investigator who does not have a Tome, but does have 1 or more Spells, loses 1 Sanity.
details.clue_locations:
* The Hall School
* Y'ha-nthlei or The Unnamable

name: Pets and Wildlife Mutilated!
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Pets and Wildlife Mutilated! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: Monsters must be on the prowl. A monster appears in the Merchant District streets and the Southside streets.

details.description: Pickpocket Ring at Large! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Each investigator must make a Luck (-1) check. Any investigator that fails must either discard his most expensive item or lose all of his money.

details.description: Picnickers Panic! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: A careless picnicker unleashes 2 monsters on the Downtown streets.

details.description: Plague of Frogs is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Lore checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Will checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. Witches and Warlocks move like stalkers.

details.description: Plague of Insects is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators that end their turns in the street lose 1 Stamina and 1 Sanity. The terror level increases by 1.
details.flavor_text: The strange locusts and beetles around town have reached a critical mass! They crawl, fly and hop everywhere.

details.description: Planetary Alignment is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Thanks to the mystic energy generated by the planetary alignment, all Spells have a Sanity cost of 0.

details.description: Plans in Motion is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.ongoing_effect: At the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after this card enters play), roll a die. On a 1 or 2, add one uprising token to the Deep Ones Rising track.
details.pass_condition: If the Feds Raid Innsmouth track is filled, resolve it, then return this card to the box.
details.fail_condition: If the Ancient One awakens, return this card to the box.
details.flavor_text: Strange schemes are afoot in the city of Innsmouth, preparing the way for the Ancient One's awakening.

details.description: Police Helpless to Displace Vagrants is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the Downtown streets may spend $5 or 2 Clue tokens to draw the top 3 cards of the Spell deck, choose 1 Spell to keep, and discard the rest.

name: Police Offer Reward for Information!
gate_location: Esoteric Order of Dagon or The Witch House

details.description: Police Offer Reward for Information! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Each investigator may spend any number of his Clue tokens. For each Clue token he spends, he gains $2.

details.description: Police Proclaim Cult Crackdown is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All Cultists in Arkham are returned to the cup.
details.flavor_text: The police say that the recent rise in cultist activity cannot be tolerated, and call on all citizens to report sightings of "people in strange garb or performing rituals."

name: Police Step Up Patrols in Northside!

details.page_title: Police Step Up Patrols in Northside!
details.description: Police Step Up Patrols in Northside! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Northside streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Private Security Hired in Uptown! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Uptown streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Profane Relics Seized is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 1 Clue token on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play).
details.pass_condition: If an investigator with either a Silver Twilight Lodge Membership or a "One of the Thousand" Cult Membership (from the The Black Goat of the Woods expansion) discards 3 Clue tokens while at the South Church, return this card to the box. Each investigator then gains 1 Unique Item.
details.fail_condition:
If there are 6 Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box, draw 2 Unique Items per investigator and return them to the box.

Activity At: South Church

details.description: Raining Cats and Dogs is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Speed checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty, and players receive one less movement point during the Movement Phase. Sneak checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. Return any Fire Vampires in play to the cup. If a Fire Vampire enters play, return it to the cup and draw a different monster.

name: Rare Antiquities Up for Auction!
gate_location: Wizard's Hill or The Witch House

details.description: Rare Antiquities Up for Auction! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first player draws 1 Unique Item. He may purchase it for the list price. Each time a player chooses not to purchase it, the player to his left may purchase it, but the price goes up by $1. Once each player has had a chance to buy the item, if no one has purchased it, the item is discarded.

details.description: Rash of Graffiti is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Skill checks to close or seal gates in Arkham are made at a -2 penalty.
details.flavor_text: Business and homeowners alike have reported an increase in graffiti around Arkham. The perpetrators seem to have a penchant for yellow paint. They believe that some ne'er-do-wells have come into a few cases of the stuff and are just having trouble figuring out what to do with it. But could it be something more sinister?

name: Rash of Missing Persons!
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Rash of Missing Persons! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: The first player chooses an investigator to be lost in time and space

details.description: Relentless Doom is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, monster surges cause 1 doom token to be added to the Ancient One's doom track.

details.description: Restful Day! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All investigators gain 1 Stamina.

details.description: Return to the Old Ways is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Curse of the Dark Pharaoh expansion.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 1 Clue token on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase after the first. Any time a player attempts to use a Common Item, whether by applying its bonuses to combat, exhausting it, or discarding it, roll a die. On a 1 or 2, it falls apart before it can be used and must be discarded.
details.pass_condition: If a player discards a Common Item while in an Other World, return this card to the box. Each player gains 2 Clue tokens.
details.fail_condition: If there are 5 Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. All Common Items in play are discarded, and the Common Item deck is removed from the game.

name: Ring Around the Moon
gate_location: Esoteric Order of Dagon or Independence Square

details.description: Ring Around the Moon is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Each time an investigator in Arkham fails a Spell check, add a doom token to the doom track.

details.description: Riots is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: When this card enters play, place 3 Stamina tokens per player on it. Any player may pay Stamina while in the Northside streets during the Arkham Encounters Phase to remove Stamina tokens from this card on a 1-for-1 basis.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 1 Stamina token per player on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play).
details.pass_condition: If there are no Stamina tokens on this card, return it to the box. Each player draws 1 Spell.
details.fail_condition: If there are 6 Stamina tokens per player on this card, return it to the box. From now on, it costs investigators 2 movement points to enter a street area instead of 1.

sections.Errata: While this card refers to a Hunter in Darkness monster, no such monster appears to exist, either in the original Arkham Horror or in the expansions. The text should probably be taken to refer to the Haunter of the Dark, a Mask monster.

sections.Clarification: This event does not affect investigators in the Other Worlds.

details.description: The Great Ritual is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game
details.ongoing_effect: Cultists, Witches, Warlocks, and High Priests have their toughness increased by 2 while this card is in play. Place 1 clue token on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play).
details.pass_condition: If a single player discards 3 spells (4 if there are 5 or more players) while in the French Hill streets during the Arkham Encounters phase, return this card to the box. Each player gains 2 clue tokens.
details.fail_condition: If there are ever 5 Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. From now on, draw 2 Mythos cards each Mythos Phase. Ignore everything on the first card except for the gate opening.

details.description: The Innsmouth Plague is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.ongoing_effect: At the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after this card enters play), the first player draws one Innsmouth Look card.
details.pass_condition: If an investigator discards 2 Spells during the Arkham Encounters Phase while in the Church Green streets, return this card to the box.
details.fail_condition: If an investigator gets the Innsmouth Look and is devoured, return this card to the box.
details.flavor_text: A terrible plague begins to spread from the city of Innsmouth.

details.description: The Key and the Gate is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.ongoing_effect: Each time a Gate opens, place a Clue token on this card.
details.pass_condition: Investigators who end their movement in the Southside streets can discard two Gate trophies to return this card to the box and gain 2 Clue tokens.
details.fail_condition: When there are as many Clue tokens on this card as there are investigators plus one, return this card to the box and draw a new Mythos card. Treat any Gates opened by that Mythos card as Gate bursts and ignore the rest of the card.

details.description: The Man in Black is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators who end their movement in the French Hill streets may deal with the Man in Black to gain power. They roll dice equal to their current Sanity and for every failed die, the lose 1 Sanity. If this reduces them to 0 Sanity, they are devoured and the player must start a new character. Otherwise, they gain 1 Clue token and draw 1 Spell.

details.description: The Next Act Begins! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability:
The terror level is increased by 1. However, no gate is opened this turn.

The next act of the charity performance of The King in Yellow has begun.

The first player must put the top card of the Act deck into play.

The first player gains 1 Clue token.

details.mythos_ability:
The terror level is increased by 1. However, no gate is opened this turn.

details.description: The Rails are Washed Out is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators cannot travel between cities using the Train Station or other depot locations while this card remains in play.

details.description: The Repairer of Reputations is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Players who are Cursed, have an outstanding bank loan, possess a Detriment card, or are in jail may immediately discard the offending cards and/or may be immediately freed from jail. Those who take advantage of this offer are reduced to 0 Sanity.
details.flavor_text: Signs are place in conspicuous locations, advertising "Mr. Wilde, Repairer of Reputations."

sections.Mythos Source: The Repairer of Reputations is a short story featured in the anthology The King in Yellow, written by Robert W. Chambers (1895).

details.description: The Siren Call! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: A strange feeling pulls the first player towards the sea, forcing him to draw two Innsmouth Look cards.

details.description: The Southside Strangler Strikes is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.ongoing_effect: Return one Ally from the Ally deck to the box at random at the end of every Mythos Phase while this card is in play (beginning the turn after it entered play). The Southside Strangler has struck again!
details.pass_condition: If a single player discards 5 Clue tokens while in Ma's Boarding House during the Arkham Encounters Phase, return this card to the box. Each player receives a $5 reward from the police.
details.fail_condition: If there are no more Allies to return to the box at the end of the Mythos Phase, return this card to the box. Each player must lower either their maximum Sanity or Stamina (their choice) by 1 for the rest of the game.

sections.Misprint: The Pass condition should be resolved in the Southside Streets instead of Ma's Boarding House.

details.description: The Stars Are Right is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.ongoing_effect: Roll a die at the end of every Mythos Phase while this card is in play (beginning the turn after it enters play). On a 1 or 2, place one doom token on the ancient one's doom track.
details.pass_condition: If a player discards an Ally card while in the Downtown streets during the Arkham Encounter Phase, return this card to the box. Each player draws 2 Common Items.
details.fail_condition: If the Ancient One awakens, return this card to the box.

details.description: The Story Continues… is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: Shuffle the mythos deck, being sure to include this card in it. After the deck is shuffled, draw a new mythos card for the turn.
details.flavor_text: "I cannot remember, I must not remember what I saw on that dark and foreboding night.."

details.description: The Streets are Flooded is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All street areas are also aquatic locations while this card remains in play.

details.description: The Tattered King is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 1 Clue token on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it enters play).
details.fail_condition: When the 5th clue token is place on this card, return this card to the box. Any investigator in a street area is devoured, as the Tattered King catches sight of him in the dead of night.

details.description: The Tempest is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Luck checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Speed checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus, and investigators receive 1 extra movement point. All flying monsters in the Sky when this card enters play, or that move to the Sky while it remains in play, are returned to the monster cup.

details.description: The Terrible Experiment is a mythos card. If first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: When this card enters play, place 5 monsters from the cup on it. Any player may choose to fight one or more of these monsters while in the Miskatonic U. streets during the Arkham Encounter Phase. If defeated, they are claimed as monster trophies. These monsters do not move, are not considered on the board, and do not count against the monster limit.
details.ongoing_effect: Place a monster on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it entered play).
details.pass_condition: If there are no monsters on this card, return it to the box. Each player draws 1 Skill.
details.fail_condition: If there are 8 monsters on this card, return it to the box. Raise the terror level to 10 and place the monsters that were on it in play in the Miskatonic U. streets.

sections.Clarification: Any effects which would affect the monsters in the Miskatonic U. neighborhood do not apply to those placed on this card.

details.description: The Trembling Earth is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.

While this card is in play, investigators receive 1 less movement point each turn.

details.pass_condition: When there are 6 Clue tokens on this rumor, return it to the box and each investigator gains 1 Spell.
details.fail_condition: When there are 0 Clue tokens on this rumor, return it to the box and each investigator loses 2 Sanity.

details.flavor_text: The playhouse downtown has been struggling for months, and the recent run of the infamous play, "The King in Yellow," may revitalize it. But at what cost to those who watch it?
