details.description: To Conquer Death is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: When this card enters play, place 3 Clue tokens on it. Each time an investigator passes a Horror check with 2 or more successes, add 1 Clue token to this card.
details.ongoing_effect: During the Mythos Phase, remove 1 Clue token from this card.
details.pass_condition: If the number of Clue tokens on this card is equal to the number of investigators plus 5, return this card to the box. Each investigator has his maximum Stamina increased by 1. Any player with 1 or more Injury cards (from the Dunwich Horror expansion) may choose 1 to discard.
details.fail_condition: If there are no Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. All monsters have the Endless ability and Undead monsters no longer count towards the monster limit.

name: Tongue-Tied Teacher Taunted!
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Tongue-Tied Teacher Taunted! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: Each investigator with the most Clue tokens loses 1 Clue token. Each investigator with the fewest Clue tokens gains 1 Clue token.

details.description: Tourists Flock to Arkham is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, investigators in a street area receive -1 on all Combat checks and gain 1 Sanity each time they pass a Combat check.

details.description: Town Welcomes Visitors is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first player chooses a dimensional symbol. Draw new monsters from the monster cup to replace each monster with that dimensional symbol in Arkham. (Ignore monsters in the Sky, but not the Outskirts.) Return monsters so replaced to the cup after all new monsters have been drawn.

name: Train Robbers Strike Again!
gate_location: Esoteric Order of Dagon or Independence Square

details.description: Train Robbers Strike Again! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Any investigator currently in the Train Station or a depot location an an expansion game board must discard 2 items, if able.
details.clue_locations:
* Artists' Colony
* Devil Reef or The Unnamable

details.description: Traveller Lost in Fog! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first player must immediately move to the Strange High House in the Mist.

details.description: Two Sunsets? is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Sneak and Will checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty
details.flavor_text: A blurring effect in the atmosphere, which scientists believe may be caused by foreign weapons testing, has caused there to seem to be two suns, setting in rapid succession!

details.description: Unending Darkness is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 10 Clue tokens on this card. When a monster moves from a street area or a location to the Sky or into a vortex (either on the Dunwich Horror or Innsmouth Horror expansion game boards), remove 1 Clue token from this card.
details.pass_condition: If each investigator is Blessed, return this card to the box. Each investigator has his maximum Sanity increased by 1. Any player with 1 or more Madness cards (from the Dunwich Horror expansion) may choose 1 to discard.
details.fail_condition: If the number of Clue tokens on this card is less than the terror level, return it to the box. All players discard all of their Spells and then the Spell deck is returned to the box. Investigators may not use Spells for the rest of the game.

details.description: Unpleasant Surprise! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: If the terror level is 8 or higher, or there is only one empty space left on the Ancient One's doom track, then the Ancient One immediately awakens.

details.description: Unusual Moonrises is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Sneak and Lore checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus. Will checks in Arkham are made at a -2 penalty.
details.flavor_text: The moon seems overly large, and is close to the horizon from dusk to dawn, casting long shadows throughout the entire night. The scientists at Miskatonic University believe it to be an optical illusion.

details.description: Unusual Phenomenon Sighted in Arkham! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The first player must choose one inactive rift (if there are any). That rift's track immediately fills up and the rift opens.

details.description: Uptown Hosts Famous Publisher is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators in Arkham may immediately make a Will (-1) check. Any who pass may pay $3 to draw Spells as if they were receiving Magic Lessons at Ye Olde Magick Shoppe
details.flavor_text: The Uptown Literati are hosting F. Tennyson Neely, British publisher of "the King in Yellow," at Ye Olde Magick Shoppe. Only the most elite are invited to hear him speak.

details.description: Vigilante Guards the Night! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror board game.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Downtown streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Virulent Disease is a mythos card. It first appeared in The King in Yellow expansion.
details.ongoing_effect: Place 1 Clue token on this card at the end of every Mythos Phase (beginning the turn after it enters play. If a player ends his movement at St. Mary's Hospital, he may place a doom token on his investigator sheet.
details.fail_condition: When the 3rd clue token is place on this card, return this card to the box and discard the doom tokens from the investigator sheets. All investigators who discard doom tokens are unaffected by the disease. Investigators who do not discard doom tokens are reduced to 1 Stamina and 1 Sanity due to the disease's ferocity.

name: Vivisection Victim!
expansion: The Black Goat of the Woods

details.description: Vivisection Victim! is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Black Goat of the Woods expansion.
details.edition: The Black Goat of the Woods
details.mythos_ability: The first player may choose a monster in Arkham and move it to the Science Building.

details.description: Weakened Forces is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Innsmouth Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, all Magical Weapons have any bonuses they provide to Combat checks reduced by 1.

details.description: Whateley Family Feud Escalates! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: All monsters in the Backwoods Country streets or locations are returned to the cup.

details.description: Whateleys Accused of Witchcraft! is a mythos card.It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: The accusations come after someone (or something) releases 2 monsters into the Backwoods Country streets.

details.description: Whippoorwills of Dunwich is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: If an investigator is reduced to 0 Stamina or Sanity while this card is in play, roll a die. On a 1, the investigator is devoured as the whippoorwills catch his soul, and the terror level is increased by 1.

details.description: Wildfire is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Each investigator in the Black Cave or Woods at the end of the Mythos Phase, as well as each investigator in Bishop's Brook Bridge, Cold Spring Glen, or Wizard's Hill on the Dunwich Horror expansion game board at the end of the Mythos Phase, loses 1 Stamina.
details.clue_locations: Cold Spring Glen or Hibb's Roadhouse

details.description: Wild Visions Plague City! is a mythos card.It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators may set their skill sliders however they want when this card is drawn.

details.description: Windy is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Dunwich Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Sneak checks in Arkham are made at a -1 penalty. Speed checks in Arkham are made at a +1 bonus, and investigators receive 1 extra movement point. The Sky is considered to be adjacent to all unstable locations as well as all street areas.

details.description: Witch Burning Anniversary! is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Arkham Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: An ancient curse strikes Arkham, releasing 2 monsters into the Rivertown streets.

details.description: Wolfsbane in Bloom is a mythos card. It first appeared in the Miskatonic Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Monsters with Magical Immunity are treated as having Magical Resistance instead and have their toughness increased by 1.
details.clue_locations: Esoteric Order of Dagon or Science Building

details.description: World Torn Asunder is a mythos card.It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: When this card enters play, place 1 Clue token on it. Any player may discard a Blessing and a Skill while in the Miskatonic U. streets during the Arkham Encounter Phase to remove all Clue tokens from this card.
details.ongoing_effect: Each time monsters with the moon dimensional symbol move, place a Clue token on this card.
details.pass_condition: If there are no Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. Each investigator is restored to full Sanity and Stamina.
details.fail_condition: If there are 3 Clue tokens on this card, return it to the box. All 3 Rift tracks immediately fill up and all 3 rifts open.

sections.Clarification: Draw 3 Mythos Cards (discarding any that do not depict gates and re-drawing) to determine where the rifts appear.

details.description: Worlds Drifting Apart is a mythos card.It first appeared in the Kingsport Horror expansion.
details.mythos_ability: While this card is in play, no rift tokens may be placed on the Rift track.

details.description: Yuggoth in Retrograde is a mythos card. It first appeared in The Lurker at the Threshold expansion.
details.mythos_ability: Investigators receive +1 to Fight and Lore checks to close Gates.
